Real browser on a real TLS --public gateway (throwaway :18443, this worktree's code, default reaper timings): every terminal WS closes code=1006 and the delta SSE errors on an exact ~30s cadence (t+26.8s/57.4s/88.0s), reconnecting… caption flashing each wave — 3 times in a 100s hold — despite 10s pings flowing. Mechanism isolated: https.Server emits 'connection' with the RAW TCP socket but req.socket is the TLSSocket, so the reaper's header deadline armed on the raw socket is never disarmed by 'request'/'upgrade' and destroys every TLS connection at headerMs (30s); plain-HTTP control unaffected (probe: HTTPS SSE reaped at 1513ms with headerMs=1500, HTTP survived).

verdict.note: Real browser on a real TLS --public gateway (throwaway :18443, this worktree's code, default reaper timings): every terminal WS closes code=1006 and the delta SSE errors on an exact ~30s cadence (t+26.8s/57.4s/88.0s), reconnecting… caption flashing each wave — 3 times in a 100s hold — despite 10s pings flowing. Mechanism isolated: https.Server emits 'connection' with the RAW TCP socket but req.socket is the TLSSocket, so the reaper's header deadline armed on the raw socket is never disarmed by 'request'/'upgrade' and destroys every TLS connection at headerMs (30s); plain-HTTP control unaffected (probe: HTTPS SSE reaped at 1513ms with headerMs=1500, HTTP survived). curl control through the same gateway: actively-pinging SSE severed at 30.07s.

Same scenario, fixed reaper, freshly-restarted TLS gateway (reaper runs in the supervisor/gateway process — a hot child reload is NOT enough): 150s real-browser hold with a live session terminal open and WS/EventSource instrumented — zero WS closes, zero SSE errors, reconnecting… caption never shown (was 3 waves per 100s). curl control: actively-pinging SSE through the same gateway survived a full 75s client-side-timeout hold (was severed at 30.07s). Unit suite now exercises http AND https: slow-loris, idle keep-alive, handshake-stall all reaped at deadline; active SSE never reaped — 7/7 pass.
